days.title: Week 6 Day 1: Searching for a Place

words.example_sentence_target: Je cherche un appartement à louer.
words.example_sentence_native: I am looking for an apartment to rent.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Zhe sher-sh un ah-par-te-mon ah loo-ay.

words.example_sentence_native: This house is very big.

words.example_sentence_target: J'ai vu une annonce sur internet.
words.example_sentence_native: I saw an ad on the internet.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Zhay voo oon ah-nons soor in-ter-net.

words.example_sentence_target: Le studio est parfait pour une personne.
words.example_sentence_native: The studio is perfect for one person.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Le stew-dee-oh ay par-fay poor oon per-son.

words.example_sentence_native: When can I visit the apartment?

words.example_sentence_target: Est-ce que le logement est encore disponible ?
words.example_sentence_native: Is the accommodation still available?
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Es-ke le lozh-mon ay on-kor dees-po-neeb-luh ?

sentences.target: Je voudrais prendre rendez-vous pour une visite.
sentences.native: I would like to make an appointment for a viewing.
sentences.phonetic: Zhe voo-dray pron-druh ron-day-voo poor oon vee-zeet.

sentences.target: Quel est le montant du loyer ?
sentences.phonetic: Kel ay le mon-ton doo lwa-yay ?

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Bonjour, je vous appelle pour l'annonce de l'appartement.
lines.native: Hello, I'm calling about the apartment ad.
lines.phonetic: Bon-zhoor, zhe vooz ah-pel poor lah-nons de la-par-te-mon.

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Oui, il est toujours disponible. Voulez-vous le visiter ?
lines.native: Yes, it is still available. Do you want to visit it?
lines.phonetic: Wee, eel ay too-zhoor dees-po-neeb-luh. Voo-lay-voo le vee-zee-tay ?

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Oui, demain à 14h, c'est possible ?
lines.native: Yes, is tomorrow at 2 PM possible?
lines.phonetic: Wee, de-man ah ka-tor-zer, say po-seeb-luh ?

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: D'accord, c'est noté. Voici l'adresse.
lines.native: Okay, noted. Here is the address.
lines.phonetic: Da-kor, say no-tay. Vwa-see la-dres.

days.cultural_note: In France, 'le loyer' (rent) is usually paid monthly. Most landlords will ask for a 'garant' (guarantor) who can pay the rent if you cannot.
days.pronunciation_tip: In 'appartement', the 'ent' at the end is nasal, like the 'on' in 'song' but shorter.

days.title: Week 6 Day 2: Renting and Contracts

words.example_sentence_target: Le loyer est de 800 euros par mois.
words.example_sentence_native: The rent is 800 euros per month.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Le lwa-yay ay de wee-son er-oh par mwa.

words.example_sentence_target: Je dois signer le bail aujourd'hui.
words.example_sentence_native: I have to sign the lease today.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Zhe dwa seen-yay le bye oh-zhoor-dwee.

words.example_sentence_target: La caution est égale à un mois de loyer.
words.example_sentence_native: The deposit is equal to one month's rent.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: La ko-syon ay ay-gal ah un mwa de lwa-yay.

words.example_sentence_native: The landlord lives on the ground floor.

words.example_sentence_target: Veuillez signer ici, s'il vous plaît.
words.example_sentence_native: Please sign here, please.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Ver-yay seen-yay ee-see, seel voo play.

words.example_sentence_target: Est-ce que les charges sont comprises ?
words.example_sentence_native: Are the utilities included?

sentences.target: J'ai besoin d'une quittance de loyer.
sentences.phonetic: Zhay be-zwan doon kee-tons de lwa-yay.

sentences.target: Le contrat commence le premier du mois.
sentences.native: The contract starts on the first of the month.
sentences.phonetic: Le kon-tra ko-mons le pre-mee-ay doo mwa.

dialogues.context: Talking to the landlord about the contract.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Quel est le montant de la caution ?
lines.native: What is the amount of the deposit?
lines.phonetic: Kel ay le mon-ton de la ko-syon ?

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: C'est un mois de loyer hors charges.
lines.native: It's one month's rent excluding charges.
lines.phonetic: Say un mwa de lwa-yay or sharzh.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: D'accord. Quand puis-je signer le bail ?
lines.native: Okay. When can I sign the lease?
lines.phonetic: Da-kor. Kon pwee-zhe seen-yay le bye ?

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Dès que vous avez les documents nécessaires.
lines.native: As soon as you have the necessary documents.
lines.phonetic: Day ke vooz ah-vay lay do-koo-mon nay-say-sair.

multipleChoice.question: What is 'the landlord'?
multipleChoice.explanation: 'Le propriétaire' is the owner or landlord.

days.cultural_note: A 'quittance de loyer' is a very important document in France. It serves as proof of address for many administrative procedures.

days.title: Week 6 Day 3: Utilities and Bills
days.title_native: Semaine 6 Jour 3 : Services publics et factures

words.example_sentence_target: Je dois ouvrir un compte pour l'électricité.
words.example_sentence_native: I need to open an account for electricity.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Zhe dwa oo-vreer un kont poor lay-lek-tree-see-tay.

words.example_sentence_target: L'eau est comprise dans le loyer.
words.example_sentence_native: Water is included in the rent.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Lo ay kom-preez don le lwa-yay.

words.example_sentence_target: Il y a du gaz pour la cuisine ?
words.example_sentence_native: Is there gas for cooking?
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Eel ee ah doo gaz poor la kwee-zeen ?

words.example_sentence_native: I want to install internet tomorrow.

words.example_sentence_target: J'ai reçu la facture de téléphone.
words.example_sentence_native: I received the phone bill.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Zhay re-soo la fak-toor de tay-lay-fon.

words.example_sentence_native: The heating is electric.

sentences.native: I would like to pay my bill.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Bonjour, je voudrais souscrire un contrat d'électricité.
lines.native: Hello, I would like to sign up for an electricity contract.
lines.phonetic: Bon-zhoor, zhe voo-dray soos-kreer un kon-tra day-lek-tree-see-tay.

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: D'accord. Quelle est votre adresse ?
lines.native: Okay. What is your address?
lines.phonetic: Da-kor. Kel ay vo-tra dres ?

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: C'est le 10 rue de la Paix.
lines.native: It's 10 rue de la Paix.
lines.phonetic: Say le dees roo de la pay.

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Très bien. Je crée votre compte.
lines.native: Very well. I am creating your account.
lines.phonetic: Tray bee-an. Zhe kray vo-truh kont.

days.cultural_note: In France, you usually pay for electricity and gas separately from the rent unless it's a shared house or a specific short-term rental.
days.pronunciation_tip: 'Eau' is pronounced exactly like the letter 'O' in English.

days.title: Week 6 Day 4: Moving In

words.example_sentence_native: Here are the keys to the apartment.
I am going to move in next Saturday.

words.example_sentence_target: J'ai beaucoup de cartons à porter.
words.example_sentence_native: I have a lot of boxes to carry.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Zhay bo-koo de kar-ton ah por-tay.

words.example_sentence_target: Nous devons faire l'état des lieux.
words.example_sentence_native: We must do the inventory walkthrough.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Noo de-von fair lay-tah day lee-er.

words.example_sentence_target: Est-ce qu'il y a un ascenseur ?
words.example_sentence_native: Is there an elevator?
The apartment is on the third floor.

sentences.native: Where can I park the truck?

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Bonjour, je suis prêt pour l'état des lieux.
lines.native: Hello, I am ready for the inventory walkthrough.
lines.phonetic: Bon-zhoor, zhe swee pray poor lay-tah day lee-er.

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Parfait. Regardons chaque pièce ensemble.
lines.native: Perfect. Let's look at each room together.
lines.phonetic: Par-fay. Re-gar-don shak pee-es on-som-bluh.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Tout semble correct. Voici ma signature.
lines.native: Everything seems correct. Here is my signature.
lines.phonetic: Too som-bluh ko-rekt. Vwa-see ma seen-ya-toor.

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Merci. Voici vos clés. Bienvenue chez vous !
lines.native: Thank you. Here are your keys. Welcome home!
lines.phonetic: Mair-see. Vwa-see vo klay. Bee-an-ve-noo shay voo !

days.cultural_note: The 'état des lieux' is a detailed document describing the condition of the apartment. It's crucial for getting your deposit back later!

days.title: Week 6 Day 5: Problems and Repairs

words.example_sentence_target: Il y a une fuite d'eau dans la cuisine.
words.example_sentence_native: There is a water leak in the kitchen.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Eel ee ah oon fweet do don la kwee-zeen.

words.example_sentence_native: The heating is broken.
Can you repair the shower?
I am calling a plumber immediately.
The window is broken.
I have to change the lightbulb.

sentences.target: Il n'y a plus d'eau chaude.
sentences.native: There is no more hot water.
sentences.phonetic: Eel nee ah ploo do shod.

dialogues.context: Calling the landlord about a problem.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Bonjour, j'ai un problème dans mon appartement.
lines.native: Hello, I have a problem in my apartment.
lines.phonetic: Bon-zhoor, zhay un pro-blem don mon ah-par-te-mon.

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Qu'est-ce qui ne va pas ?
lines.native: What is wrong?
lines.phonetic: Kes-kee ne va pah ?

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Il y a une fuite sous l'évier.
lines.native: There is a leak under the sink.
lines.phonetic: Eel ee ah oon fweet soo lay-vee-ay.

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: D'accord, j'envoie un plombier cet après-midi.
lines.native: Okay, I'm sending a plumber this afternoon.
lines.phonetic: Da-kor, zhon-vwa un plom-bee-ay set ah-pray-mee-dee.

multipleChoice.question: What do you say if the heating is not working?
multipleChoice.explanation: 'En panne' means it is out of order or broken.

days.cultural_note: In France, major repairs are the responsibility of the 'propriétaire' (landlord), while small daily maintenance is for the 'locataire' (tenant).
days.pronunciation_tip: 'Panne' rhymes with 'man' but with a softer 'a'.

days.title: Week 6 Day 6: Furniture and Appliances

words.example_sentence_native: I need a new bed.

words.example_sentence_target: La table est dans la cuisine.
words.example_sentence_native: The table is in the kitchen.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: La tah-bluh ay don la kwee-zeen.

words.example_sentence_native: A chair is missing here.
The sofa is very comfortable.
The fridge is empty.
Where is the washing machine?

sentences.native: I am looking for second-hand furniture.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Combien coûte cette table ?
lines.native: How much does this table cost?
lines.phonetic: Kom-bee-an koot set tah-bluh ?

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Elle est à 50 euros.
lines.native: It's 50 euros.
lines.phonetic: El ay ah sin-kont er-oh.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Est-ce que vous faites la livraison ?
lines.native: Do you do delivery?
lines.phonetic: Es-ke voo fayt la lee-vray-zon ?

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Oui, pour 10 euros de plus.
lines.native: Yes, for 10 euros more.
lines.phonetic: Wee, poor deez er-oh de ploo.

days.cultural_note: Websites like 'Le Bon Coin' are very popular in France for buying second-hand furniture and appliances directly from people in your neighborhood.

days.title: Week 6 Day 7: Neighbors and Community

words.example_sentence_native: My neighbor is very kind.
My neighbor helped me.
I like this neighborhood a lot.

words.example_sentence_target: Il y a trop de bruit ici.
words.example_sentence_native: There is too much noise here.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Eel ee ah tro de brwee ee-see.

words.example_sentence_native: You must respect the neighbors.
It's a very quiet building.

sentences.native: Can I borrow some salt from you?

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Bonjour, je suis votre nouveau voisin.
lines.native: Hello, I am your new neighbor.
lines.phonetic: Bon-zhoor, zhe swee vo-truh noo-vo vwa-zan.

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Ah, enchanté ! Je m'appelle Paul.
lines.native: Ah, nice to meet you! My name is Paul.
lines.phonetic: Ah, on-shon-tay ! Zhe ma-pel Pol.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Moi c'est John. J'habite au deuxième.
lines.native: I'm John. I live on the second floor.
lines.phonetic: Mwa say Jon. Zha-beet oh der-zee-em.

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Bienvenue dans l'immeuble !
lines.native: Welcome to the building!
lines.phonetic: Bee-an-ve-noo don lee-mer-bluh !

days.cultural_note: The 'Fête des Voisins' (Neighbors' Day) is a big event in France where people gather in their building or street for food and drinks to get to know each other.
days.pronunciation_tip: 'Voisin' ends with a nasal sound, while 'Voisine' ends with a clear 'n' sound because of the 'e'.
